items.title: Cloud credentials directory created without mode 0700 — API tokens world-listable on multi-user systems
items.intent: The cloud credentials directory must not be listable by other users — it stores API tokens and must be created with mode 0700 so directory contents are not exposed, matching the protection applied to credentials.ts file itself.
items.description: src/cloud/provisioning/credentials.ts line 53 calls mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true }) without a mode option when creating the cloud/ directory. This creates the directory with default permissions (0755), making it world-readable. While credentials.json is written with mode 0600 (correct), any user on the system can ls the cloud/ directory to enumerate that cloud credentials exist. Same pattern: registry.ts line 57 and snapshot-registry.ts line 51 both call mkdirSync without mode. registry.ts is already covered by BUG-077. snapshot-registry.ts stores non-sensitive data (provider, region, version, timestamp) but should be consistent. Fix: pass { recursive: true, mode: 0o700 } to all three mkdirSync calls.
steps: Read src/cloud/provisioning/credentials.ts lines 50-55 — confirm mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true }) has no mode option
Change mkdirSync in credentials.ts to mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true, mode: 0o700 })
Read src/cloud/provisioning/snapshot-registry.ts lines 48-53 — confirm mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true }) has no mode option
Change mkdirSync in snapshot-registry.ts to mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true, mode: 0o700 })
Run credentials tests and snapshot-registry tests — confirm both pass with no regressions
acceptanceCriteria: The cloud/ directory (deployDir/cloud/) is created with mode 0700 — stat confirms no group/other read bits
snapshot-registry parent directory is created with mode 0700
File permissions on credentials.json and snapshots.json remain unchanged at 0600
items.groomingNotes: PO AC FIX (2026-03-23). AC item 3 corrected: 'remain unchanged at 0600' was factually wrong — snapshots.json is currently created at 0644, not 0600. Steps 10-12 (added by Tech Lead Pass 3) introduce the fix. AC now accurately states snapshots.json is a new 0600 target, not a pre-existing one. Previous failure (failCount=1) may have been caused by the verifier checking for an 'unchanged' 0600 when the file was actually 0644. readyForSprint=true confirmed — steps and AC are now aligned.

items.title: Export bundle directory and archive file created without secure modes — agent data world-readable on multi-user systems
items.intent: The export bundle output directory must be created with mode 0700 and the bundle archive must be written with mode 0600 — the export contains agent workspace, ops data, and identity files that must not be readable by other users on multi-user systems.
items.description: src/evolve/lifecycle/export.ts line 238 calls mkdirSync(parentDir, { recursive: true }) without a mode option when creating the parent directory for the export bundle. This creates the directory with default permissions (0755), world-readable. Line 242 calls createWriteStream(bundlePath) without a mode option, creating the bundle archive with default permissions (typically 0644), world-readable. The export bundle contains workspace data, ops/audit traces, tool and skill configs, and identity files — sensitive agent data that other users on the system can read. Credentials and .env are excluded, but audit logs and conversation memory are included. Fix: add mode DIR_MODE_SECRET to mkdirSync on line 238 and chmodSync after for existing directories. Pass mode FILE_MODE_SECRET to createWriteStream via { mode: FILE_MODE_SECRET } options, then chmodSync the file after writing completes.
steps: Read src/evolve/lifecycle/export.ts lines 1-30 — confirm DIR_MODE_SECRET and FILE_MODE_SECRET are not imported (neither from node:fs nor config/defaults.js)

Read src/evolve/lifecycle/export.ts lines 185-260 — confirm mkdirSync(parentDir, { recursive: true }) at ~line 238 has no mode option, and createWriteStream(bundlePath) at ~line 242 has no mode option
Add chmodSync to the existing node:fs import in export.ts
Add import { DIR_MODE_SECRET, FILE_MODE_SECRET } from ../../config/defaults.js to export.ts
Change mkdirSync(parentDir, { recursive: true }) to mkdirSync(parentDir, { recursive: true, mode: DIR_MODE_SECRET }) in export.ts
Add chmodSync(parentDir, DIR_MODE_SECRET) on the line immediately after mkdirSync in export.ts — fixes existing directories
Change createWriteStream(bundlePath) to createWriteStream(bundlePath, { mode: FILE_MODE_SECRET }) in export.ts
After the await pipeline(...) call completes (archive is fully written), add chmodSync(bundlePath, FILE_MODE_SECRET) to fix permissions on the archive file
Run the full test suite — confirm export/lifecycle tests pass with no regressions
acceptanceCriteria: The export bundle parent directory is created with mode 0700 — stat confirms no group/other read or execute bits
The export bundle archive (.tar.gz) is written with mode 0600 — stat confirms no group/other read bits
items.groomingNotes: TECH LEAD PASS 1 (2026-03-23). New bug identified from codebase scan — same permission pattern as BUG-085 through BUG-105 series. export.ts creates the bundle parent dir with mkdirSync (no mode) and the archive with createWriteStream (no mode). Export bundle includes workspace data, ops/audit logs, and identity files. Credentials excluded but audit data and memory are included — world-readable is a direct security exposure. Steps follow the exact proven pattern: import constants, add mode to creation call, add chmodSync after for existing installs. createWriteStream supports mode option via { mode } options object. chmodSync after pipeline ensures the file mode is set even if umask overrides. Ready to sprint.

items.title: Backup: unencrypted archive created without mode 0600 and snapsDir missing chmodSync — transient exposure and existing-install gap
items.intent: The unencrypted backup archive must be created with mode 0600 to eliminate world-readable exposure during the encryption window, and chmodSync must be added after mkdirSync so existing installs with 0755 snapshots directories are fixed.
items.description: src/operate/backup/backup.ts has two permission gaps: (1) line 210 calls createWriteStream(archivePath) without a mode option, creating the unencrypted tar.gz with default permissions (0644 — world-readable). This file exists from archive creation through GPG encryption (Step 2) and is deleted in Step 5 Cleanup. On a multi-user system, another user can read the unencrypted archive during this window. (2) line 166 calls mkdirSync(snapsDir, { recursive: true, mode: DIR_MODE_SECRET }) without a following chmodSync, so on existing installs where snapshots/ was previously created at 0755, the mode is never corrected. GPG-encrypted backup files (.tar.gz.gpg) and manifest files are created by external processes or writeFile without explicit mode. Fix: (1) change createWriteStream(archivePath) to createWriteStream(archivePath, { mode: FILE_MODE_SECRET }) and add chmodSync(archivePath, FILE_MODE_SECRET) after the pipeline completes. (2) Add chmodSync(snapsDir, DIR_MODE_SECRET) after mkdirSync. Also add explicit mode to writeFile(manifestPath) for belt-and-suspenders.
steps: Read src/operate/backup/backup.ts lines 1-30 — confirm chmodSync is NOT in the node:fs import, and FILE_MODE_SECRET is NOT imported
Read src/operate/backup/backup.ts lines 155-175 — confirm mkdirSync(snapsDir, { recursive: true, mode: DIR_MODE_SECRET }) exists but has NO chmodSync call after it
Read src/operate/backup/backup.ts lines 205-215 — confirm createWriteStream(archivePath) has no mode option
Read src/operate/backup/backup.ts lines 245-255 — confirm writeFile(manifestPath, ...) has no mode option
Add chmodSync to the node:fs import in backup.ts
Add FILE_MODE_SECRET to the existing import from ../../config/defaults.js in backup.ts (DIR_MODE_SECRET is already imported)
Add chmodSync(snapsDir, DIR_MODE_SECRET) on the line immediately after mkdirSync(snapsDir, ...) — fixes existing installs where snapshots/ was created at 0755
Change createWriteStream(archivePath) to createWriteStream(archivePath, { mode: FILE_MODE_SECRET }) — restricts the unencrypted archive from creation time
After the await pipeline(...) line that writes the archive, add chmodSync(archivePath, FILE_MODE_SECRET) — enforces mode even if umask overrides
Change writeFile(manifestPath, ..., 'utf-8') to writeFile(manifestPath, ..., { encoding: 'utf-8', mode: FILE_MODE_SECRET }) — manifest contains backup metadata
Run the full test suite — confirm all backup tests pass with no regressions
acceptanceCriteria: The snapshots/ directory is created/fixed to mode 0700 — stat confirms no group/other read bits on existing installs
The unencrypted archive (.tar.gz) is created with mode 0600 — stat on a test run confirms no group/other read bits
The backup manifest (.manifest.json) is written with mode 0600
items.groomingNotes: TECH LEAD PASS 1 (2026-03-23). Two distinct gaps in same file. (1) createWriteStream without mode: unencrypted agent data world-readable during encryption window — same pattern as export.ts/BUG-106. (2) missing chmodSync after mkdirSync — same gap fixed in every bug in BUG-085..BUG-105 series. Steps follow exact proven pattern. writeFile (async) supports mode option same as writeFileSync. AC is stat-verifiable. Ready to sprint.

items.title: ops/security/ directory and sanitizer audit logs created without secure modes — quarantined input and injection attempts world-readable
items.intent: The ops/security/ directory must be created with mode 0700 and sanitizer-audit.jsonl/sanitizer-quarantine.jsonl must be written with mode 0600 — these files record quarantined content and injection attempts that must not be readable by other users.
items.description: src/secure/sanitizer/sanitizer.ts creates DEFAULT_OPS_DIR (~/.clawhq/ops/security/) on-demand via ensureDir in sanitizer/audit.ts. ensureDir calls mkdir(dir, { recursive: true }) without a mode option (line 51 of sanitizer/audit.ts), creating ops/security/ at default 0755 permissions — world-listable. The sanitizer-audit.jsonl and sanitizer-quarantine.jsonl files are written by appendFile without mode, creating them at 0644 — world-readable. ops/security/ is NOT in SCAFFOLD_DIRS_SECRET (it's a different path from the security/ root directory which is in SCAFFOLD_DIRS_SECRET). These files record security scan results including flagged PII, injection attempts, and quarantined inputs — sensitive security intelligence that other users must not read. Fix: change mkdir in sanitizer/audit.ts to pass mode DIR_MODE_SECRET, add chmodSync for existing installs, change appendFile to use mode FILE_MODE_SECRET.
steps: Read src/secure/sanitizer/audit.ts lines 1-60 — confirm mkdir(dir, { recursive: true }) at ~line 51 has no mode option, appendFile at ~line 57 has no mode option, and neither chmodSync nor DIR_MODE_SECRET nor FILE_MODE_SECRET are imported
Read src/secure/sanitizer/sanitizer.ts lines 23-40 — confirm DEFAULT_OPS_DIR is ~/.clawhq/ops/security/ (NOT ~/.clawhq/security/) and that this path is not in SCAFFOLD_DIRS_SECRET
Add chmodSync to the node:fs/promises import (or add a separate sync import from node:fs) in sanitizer/audit.ts — note: appendFile is async (fs/promises), but chmodSync is sync (node:fs)
Add import { chmodSync } from 'node:fs' to sanitizer/audit.ts (separate from the fs/promises import)
Add import { DIR_MODE_SECRET, FILE_MODE_SECRET } from ../../config/defaults.js to sanitizer/audit.ts
Change mkdir(dir, { recursive: true }) to mkdir(dir, { recursive: true, mode: DIR_MODE_SECRET }) in the ensureDir function in sanitizer/audit.ts
Add chmodSync(dir, DIR_MODE_SECRET) after the await mkdir call in ensureDir — fixes existing installs where ops/security/ was created at 0755
Change appendFile(filePath, ..., 'utf-8') to appendFile(filePath, ..., { encoding: 'utf-8', mode: FILE_MODE_SECRET }) in the appendJsonl function — this sets the mode on file creation
Run the full test suite — confirm all sanitizer tests pass with no regressions
acceptanceCriteria: ops/security/ directory is created with mode 0700 — stat confirms no group/other read or execute bits
sanitizer-audit.jsonl and sanitizer-quarantine.jsonl are created with mode 0600 — stat confirms no group/other read bits
items.groomingNotes: TECH LEAD PASS 1 (2026-03-23). New bug identified from codebase scan. ops/security/ is distinct from the security/ root (which IS in SCAFFOLD_DIRS_SECRET). The sanitizer audit path goes to ops/security/, a directory not covered by scaffold. ensureDir creates it without mode. Contains quarantined inputs (potentially including PII and injection payloads) — must priority confirmed. Steps account for the async/sync fs API split: mkdir is async (fs/promises), chmodSync must be imported separately from node:fs. appendFile mode option sets file mode on first creation. AC is stat-verifiable. Ready to sprint.
